title: Mishima inlay on thrown stoneware
notes: Incising lines into leather-hard thrown stoneware with a needle tool, then inlaying white slip into the grooves. When the slip has stiffened to bone-dry, the surface is scraped back to reveal white lines against the clay body. The piece then bisque-fires and is glazed with a transparent or semi-transparent glaze that allows the mishima lines to read through. Different from the air-dry version (published as mishima-inlay-on-air-dry-clay) — this version goes through kiln firing.
